context: This chapter describes the rituals performed during the ten days following death. It explains how pinda (rice ball) offerings made each day help form the subtle body of the departed, building limbs and organs progressively, along with the daily water and sesame offerings essential for the soul's journey.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
दशाहं यत्क्रियाकाण्डं प्रेतस्योद्देशतः प्रभो ।
तद्वदस्व महाविष्णो येन प्रेतो विमुच्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuda uvācha —
daśāhaṃ yat kriyā-kāṇḍaṃ pretasyoddeśataḥ prabho |
tadvadas-va mahā-viṣṇo yena preto vimuchyate ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: O Lord, O great Vishnu, please describe the ten days of rites performed on behalf of the departed, by which the spirit is liberated.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
शृणु वक्ष्यामि तार्क्ष्य त्वं दशाहस्य विधिक्रमम् ।
येन प्रेतशरीरस्य निर्माणं जायते क्रमात् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvācha —
śṛṇu vakṣyāmi tārkṣya tvaṃ daśāhasya vidhi-kramam |
yena preta-śarīrasya nirmāṇaṃ jāyate kramāt ||
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Listen, O Tarkhsya, I shall describe the sequence of the ten-day rites, by which the formation of the subtle body of the departed takes place progressively.

verses.hindi:
पिण्डदानं जलं चैव तिलमिश्रं दिने दिने ।
प्रेतायोद्दिश्य दातव्यं दशाहं श्रद्धया नरैः ॥
verses.transliteration:
piṇḍa-dānaṃ jalaṃ caiva tila-miśraṃ dine dine |
pretāyoddiśya dātavyaṃ daśāhaṃ śraddhayā naraiḥ ||
verses.translation: Pinda offerings and water mixed with sesame should be offered daily, dedicated to the departed soul, for ten days with faith by the relatives.

verses.hindi:
प्रथमे दिवसे पिण्डाद् शिरसो निर्माणं भवेत् ।
जलं तिलैश्च दातव्यं प्रेतस्य शिरसे हि तत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
prathame divase piṇḍād śiraso nirmāṇaṃ bhavet |
jalaṃ tilaiśca dātavyaṃ pretasya śirase hi tat ||
verses.translation: On the first day, from the pinda offering, the head of the subtle body is formed. Water and sesame should be offered — that is for the head of the departed.

verses.hindi:
द्वितीये दिवसे पिण्डात् कर्णनेत्रं तु जायते ।
नासिका मुखमेवाथ तृतीये दिवसे भवेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
dvitīye divase piṇḍāt karṇa-netraṃ tu jāyate |
nāsikā mukham evātha tṛtīye divase bhavet ||
verses.translation: On the second day, from the pinda, the ears and eyes are formed. The nose and mouth come into being on the third day.

verses.hindi:
चतुर्थे दिवसे पिण्डाद् वक्षोऽंसौ च भुजौ तथा ।
उदरं पृष्ठभागश्च पञ्चमे दिवसे भवेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
caturthe divase piṇḍād vakṣo'ṃsau ca bhujau tathā |
udaraṃ pṛṣṭha-bhāgaśca pañcame divase bhavet ||
verses.translation: On the fourth day, from the pinda, the chest, shoulders, and arms are formed. The abdomen and back come into being on the fifth day.

verses.hindi:
षष्ठे दिवसे पिण्डात् कटिदेशो भवेत् ततः ।
सप्तमे दिवसे पिण्डात् ऊरू जङ्घे भवेत् ततः ॥
verses.transliteration:
ṣaṣṭhe divase piṇḍāt kaṭi-deśo bhavet tataḥ |
saptame divase piṇḍāt ūrū jaṅghe bhavet tataḥ ||
verses.translation: On the sixth day, from the pinda, the waist region is formed. On the seventh day, from the pinda, the thighs and shanks come into being.

verses.hindi:
अष्टमे दिवसे पिण्डात् पादौ जानू तथैव च ।
नवमे दिवसे पिण्डात् रोमाणि नखसंयुतम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
aṣṭame divase piṇḍāt pādau jānū tathaiva ca |
navame divase piṇḍāt romāṇi nakha-saṃyutam ||
verses.translation: On the eighth day, from the pinda, the feet and knees are formed. On the ninth day, from the pinda, the body hair and nails come into being.

verses.hindi:
दशमे दिवसे पिण्डात् क्षुत्पिपासे भवेत् ततः ।
एवं दशदिनैः पिण्डैः प्रेतदेहो विनिर्मितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
daśame divase piṇḍāt kṣut-pipāse bhavet tataḥ |
evaṃ daśa-dinaiḥ piṇḍaiḥ preta-deho vinirmitaḥ ||
verses.translation: On the tenth day, from the pinda, hunger and thirst arise. Thus, through the pinda offerings of ten days, the body of the departed spirit is fully formed.

verses.hindi:
अन्नं वस्त्रं जलं छायां यस्तस्मै दशगात्रके ।
प्रेताय दत्तं तत्सर्वं स्वर्गलोके प्रतिष्ठते ॥
verses.transliteration:
annaṃ vastraṃ jalaṃ chāyāṃ yastasmai daśa-gātrake |
pretāya dattaṃ tat sarvaṃ svarga-loke pratiṣṭhate ||
verses.translation: Food, clothing, water, and shelter — whatever is given to the departed during the ten-day period — all of it becomes established in the heavenly realm for the departed.

verses.hindi:
प्रातःकाले तु सम्प्राप्ते स्नात्वा शुद्धेन वाससा ।
पिण्डदानं प्रकुर्वीत तिलोदकपुरःसरम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
prātaḥ-kāle tu samprāpte snātvā śuddhena vāsasā |
piṇḍa-dānaṃ prakurvīta tilodaka-puraḥsaram ||
verses.translation: When the morning arrives, having bathed and wearing clean clothes, one should perform the pinda offering preceded by the offering of water and sesame.

verses.hindi:
तण्डुलैस्तु समायुक्तं गुडेन मधुना तथा ।
पिण्डं कृत्वा विधानेन कुशोपरि निधापयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
taṇḍulaistu samāyuktaṃ guḍena madhunā tathā |
piṇḍaṃ kṛtvā vidhānena kuśopari nidhāpayet ||
verses.translation: The pinda should be made with rice mixed with jaggery and honey, and according to the prescribed method, it should be placed upon kusha grass.

verses.hindi:
दक्षिणाभिमुखो भूत्वा प्रेतस्य नाम कीर्तयन् ।
गोत्रं चोच्चार्य विधिवत् पिण्डं दद्यात् समाहितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
dakṣiṇā-bhimukho bhūtvā pretasya nāma kīrtayan |
gotraṃ cocchārya vidhivat piṇḍaṃ dadyāt samāhitaḥ ||
verses.translation: Facing south and uttering the name of the departed, declaring the gotra (lineage), one should offer the pinda with concentration according to the prescribed rites.

verses.hindi:
पिण्डदानं विना तार्क्ष्य प्रेतदेहो न जायते ।
तस्मात्प्रयत्नतः कार्यं पिण्डदानं दिने दिने ॥
verses.transliteration:
piṇḍa-dānaṃ vinā tārkṣya preta-deho na jāyate |
tasmāt prayatnataḥ kāryaṃ piṇḍa-dānaṃ dine dine ||
verses.translation: Without the pinda offering, O Tarkhsya, the subtle body of the departed does not form. Therefore, with great effort, the pinda offering must be made day after day.

verses.hindi:
पिण्डदानं न कुर्वन्ति ये नरा मोहमास्थिताः ।
तेषां पितॄणां प्रेतत्वं शाश्वतं सम्प्रवर्तते ॥
verses.transliteration:
piṇḍa-dānaṃ na kurvanti ye narā moham āsthitāḥ |
teṣāṃ pitṝṇāṃ pretatvaṃ śāśvataṃ sampravartate ||
verses.translation: Those men who, deluded, do not make the pinda offerings — their ancestors remain in the state of restless spirits permanently.

verses.hindi:
तिलोदकं दशाहं तु त्रिसन्ध्यं दापयेद् बुधः ।
तेन तृप्तिं प्रयाति प्रेतः अतृप्तश्चेत् पुनर्भवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
tilodakaṃ daśāhaṃ tu tri-sandhyaṃ dāpayed budhaḥ |
tena tṛptiṃ prayāti pretaḥ atṛptaścet punarbhavam ||
verses.translation: The wise one should offer water with sesame three times a day for ten days. By this the departed attains satisfaction; if unsatisfied, there is rebirth.

verses.hindi:
एकैकं पिण्डमेकैकं जलपात्रं च दापयेत् ।
एकैकं दीपकं चैव प्रत्यहं प्रेतभोजनम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
ekaikaṃ piṇḍam ekaikaṃ jala-pātraṃ ca dāpayet |
ekaikaṃ dīpakaṃ caiva pratyahaṃ preta-bhojanam ||
verses.translation: One pinda, one vessel of water, and one lamp should be offered each day, along with daily food for the departed spirit.

verses.hindi:
अशौचं दशरात्रं तु सपिण्डानां विधीयते ।
स्नानदानजपाश्चैव निषिद्धा दशरात्रके ॥
verses.transliteration:
aśaucaṃ daśa-rātraṃ tu sapiṇḍānāṃ vidhīyate |
snāna-dāna-japāścaiva niṣiddhā daśa-rātrake ||
verses.translation: The period of ritual impurity for the blood relatives is prescribed as ten nights. Ceremonial bathing, charitable giving, and mantra recitation are prohibited during the ten-night period.

verses.hindi:
दशाहे यः पिण्डदानं श्रद्धया कुरुते नरः ।
तेन सम्पूर्णदेहोऽसौ प्रेतो याति यमालयम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
daśāhe yaḥ piṇḍa-dānaṃ śraddhayā kurute naraḥ |
tena sampūrṇa-deho'sau preto yāti yamālayam ||
verses.translation: He who performs the pinda offerings with faith during the ten days — by that the departed, with a complete subtle body, proceeds to the abode of Yama.

verses.hindi:
दानं दशाहे यत्किञ्चित् प्रेतोद्दिश्य प्रदीयते ।
तत्सर्वं शतगुणितं प्रेतस्य उपतिष्ठति ॥
verses.transliteration:
dānaṃ daśāhe yatkiñcit pretoddiśya pradīyate |
tat sarvaṃ śata-guṇitaṃ pretasya upatiṣṭhati ||
verses.translation: Whatever gift is given during the ten days dedicated to the departed — all of it reaches the departed multiplied a hundredfold.

verses.hindi:
गोदानं हिरण्यदानं तिलदानं तथैव च ।
भूमिदानं च वस्त्रं च सप्तधान्यप्रदानकम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
go-dānaṃ hiraṇya-dānaṃ tila-dānaṃ tathaiva ca |
bhūmi-dānaṃ ca vastraṃ ca sapta-dhānya-pradānakam ||
verses.translation: The gift of a cow, the gift of gold, the gift of sesame, the gift of land, the gift of garments, and the gift of the seven grains — all these should be given.

verses.hindi:
एवं दशाहं सम्पूर्णं यः करोति विधानतः ।
तस्य प्रेतो विमुक्तात्मा देवलोकं स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
evaṃ daśāhaṃ sampūrṇaṃ yaḥ karoti vidhānataḥ |
tasya preto vimuktātmā deva-lokaṃ sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: He who thus completes the ten-day rites according to the proper procedure — his departed one, with a liberated soul, goes to the realm of the gods.
